context: Tests whether the agent correctly produces knowledge harvest artifacts from a completed issue lifecycle, including properly formatted UAT proposals (Given/When/Then), ADR-style KB entries with correct kinds and frontmatter, and a harvest.yaml with committed flags. Key instructions tested: don't duplicate prior art UAT scenarios, include issue name in committed files' frontmatter, KB entry kinds, harvest.yaml structure.

checklist.name: Given/When/Then UAT format
checklist.description: uat-proposals.md contains at least one UAT scenario written in Given/When/Then format (each scenario has a Given, When, and Then clause)

checklist.name: No prior-art duplication
checklist.description: uat-proposals.md does NOT propose scenarios that duplicate the existing prior art scenarios listed in inputs/lifecycle-state.json: 'payment-basic-flow.feature' (happy path payment) and 'payment-declined.feature' (card rejected) — new proposals must be for different scenarios

checklist.name: UAT rationale cites source
checklist.description: Each UAT proposal in uat-proposals.md includes a rationale that cites a specific lifecycle artifact (a finding ID/description, the reproduction, or a plan element) as the motivation

checklist.name: KB entry kinds used correctly
checklist.description: kb-proposals.md uses only the valid kinds: decision, pattern, anti-pattern, runbook, or postmortem — and each entry explicitly states its kind

checklist.name: Anti-pattern KB entry present
checklist.description: kb-proposals.md includes at least one entry with kind anti-pattern (the review history shows the initial plan derived the idempotency key from orderId only, which was rejected — a classic anti-pattern candidate)

checklist.name: KB ADR-style frontmatter
checklist.description: At least one KB proposal in kb-proposals.md is formatted as an ADR-style markdown file with a YAML frontmatter block (--- delimited) containing at minimum a title or kind field

checklist.name: Issue name in committed KB frontmatter
checklist.description: harvest.yaml committed KB entry bodies include an 'issue: payment-idempotency-fix' field in the frontmatter of the committed entries (so future Phase 2 lookups can trace them)

checklist.name: harvest.yaml uatProposals array
checklist.description: harvest.yaml contains a uatProposals key with an array of objects, each having at minimum: scenario (or equivalent content field), rationale, path, and committed (true or false)

checklist.name: harvest.yaml kbProposals array
checklist.description: harvest.yaml contains a kbProposals key with an array of objects, each having at minimum: kind, title, body, path, and committed (true or false)

checklist.name: Stripe TTL scenario covered
checklist.description: uat-proposals.md includes at least one scenario specifically covering the 24-hour idempotency key TTL edge case (the HIGH finding in code review that was resolved by adding a TTL-aware test)

checklist.name: Regression scenario covered
checklist.description: uat-proposals.md includes at least one scenario that covers the original regression: a retry after timeout resulting in a duplicate charge (the triage reproduction scenario)
